title: Recession Proof？ Dr Tim Predicts Aesthetics Businesses_ Health in 2021 [Aesthetics Mastery Show]
filename: Recession Proof？ Dr Tim Predicts Aesthetics Businesses_ Health in 2021 [Aesthetics Mastery Show].en.vtt
excerpt: It's quite possible that we could have a really good bounce back and end up actually in a better place in 18 months than we are now let's hope to the aesthetics mastery show last show of 2020 so we're looking forward to 2021 i'm dr sim pierce hi i'm miranda peace and today we're …

segments.text: it's quite possible that we could have a really good bounce back and end up actually in a better place in 18 months than we are now let's hope to the aesthetics mastery show last show of 2020 so we're looking forward to 2021 i'm dr sim pierce hi i'm miranda peace and today we're doing our hopes and fears for the next year ahead so before we move on let's just let me just ask you one question about this year before we put it to the grave what was your biggest learning point from 2020 um you can't be a dad and work at the same time oh really you have to just be a dad or just work okay you can't try and do both or it's like mental torment wow that's your biggest learning tell me more well it's just it's a discipline that you get into when you get a little thud at the door yeah and i don't want to be a dad who makes my kids you know afraid when they

see me so i have to switch off but if i try and work simultaneously or i don't let go of it mentally it's a form of stress that i find really uncomfortable so i've disciplined myself to just switch do one or the other but let go of whatever you're not doing let go the work when you're with your kids let go of your kids when you're with your work love it and just bouncing off that one of my biggest lessons is a similar one which is that we as business people need to let go of who what we thought our business was in the past and evolve and pivot i know pivot's been a big word of 2020 but i've been in so much admiration of all you guys doing online consultations and pivoting new extra treatments new services and not staying in that place of ego of like well you know this is you know this isn't for me i'm a clinician i actually think there's been a beautiful amount of innovation this

year yeah and it's worth reflecting on that that that usually feels scary and it's often something you've been meaning to do for a long time that you don't you haven't done for various reasons that then you're forced into doing and on the other side of it it's not that difficult and not that hard and everything's better so there's several things we've done that have actually been better because but we were afraid of doing it for years and the situation forces you into it so it's well worth noticing that for other things you might want to change in 2021 examples practical examples like putting your patients on an online system or having online booking and you know doing things through like i said online consultations those kinds of things make all the difference streamline you so let's dive in with a big elephant in the room what do you think the economy is going to be like for ascetic clinicians in 2021. well my experience that we've now gone through two big recessions in our time running skin viva limited and um mike's my experience is that there is often like a period where the breaks go on but

it's like it's psychological like people go oh can't afford that now and then about six to eight weeks later they realize they still have the problem that they don't like in terms of their appearance and your physical appearance is a human need to maintain yourself is very important and i think people basically realign their priorities and many of them will put their appearance ahead of other things and that i think is a good universal instinct for self-maintenance like i don't think that's vanity which many people will it's simply people maintaining themselves you are the fundamental unit of your success so maintain your appearance go out there find a better job recover so i'm not particularly worried even if we go into recession i think medical aesthetics will be fine the beauty industry as a whole will be fine because that's how people weigh up their needs but um that's not to say that you shouldn't accommodate in various ways or make new adjustments um if you need to if we have a recession i also think it's quite possible that we won't have a recession i think this v-shape recovery seems to be happening in most parts of the world now we are taking several hits and

who knows what's around the corner because we kind of do a recession anyway but it's quite possible that we could have a really good bounce back and end up actually in a better place in 18 months than we are now let's hope what about brexit though surely that's gonna send us over the edge yeah it's more uncertainty so absolutely it could there could be some quirky kind of supply issues with various things as possible but once again i kind of like to put myself in the position of what are you going to do as a single business owner about brexit nothing go out and market harder communicate more to your patients you know diversify find new things that you know that your clientele want serve their needs and you'll be fine you'll come out like it's even in the worst recessions you're talking a five percent shrink in the whole economy so um if we have one as a result of brexit it's only five percent it's not like a hundred percent you're going to have a business and the people who go in on the front foot you know trying to think of as many ideas as possible listening to their clients trying to do the best work they can as they always have they're going to come

out with the business at the end of it guaranteed you don't have to worry about losing everything because of recession you just need to be cleverer work harder be more creative so give us a tip for how we can boost our business in 2021 well um i mean there are so many things but attention is always number one so you've got to do things that build attention and trust simultaneously so that's how i think about marketing do something that proves your competence well but also you need someone to pay attention before you can improve your competence so what i'm seeing much more of is very high standard of before and after photography in aesthetics so this is probably the biggest shift i've seen in the last two or three years as many pages you can tell they've they're really producing their results now this is also gathers a lot of criticism because some people believe you should you shouldn't put any effort into before and after should be a completely scientific image but we also have to deal with reality which is if you're in a competing marketplace you need to present your work in as best light as possible

light as possible and you will see this once you see it it's everywhere on many of the big accounts it's about the production values of that photography so how well lit is it how good is the exposure what is the model like i mean actually selecting models that are younger and more beautiful will make a difference to how your page looks you know everyone puts this reflective stuff on lips to make them glisten in a bright light that's right near and some people crop their photos so that the after result is slightly before now the more processing the more dubious it gets from a patient perspective like what am i actually going to get but there are ways of producing a well-marketed page that don't imply that i actually think one of the ways is people who focus just on the after is quite effective because you've got a beautiful picture of lips you're not putting like a shrunk image of someone with their mouth half open with no makeup on next to it to imply that you created that difference with a needle but you are still presenting beautiful work so that might be one way can i just give my perspective from a lay perspective though i actually when i'm on instagram i want to see nice images and i want a story

and the story is from the before to the after i want there to be a victory i don't know you guys make your own decision as medical professionals but for me when i look at them i don't think i want a shyster i just think yeah nice lips and i want to ha i mean if it would be they are actually not the kind of lips that can be created that's wrong but i like a good before and after well that's the problem is that most people don't realize how much of what they're seeing is due to the production of the photography rather than what the work done with a needle and it's actually very hard even for an expert to know um and that means there's an element that's being presented um as a thing you can have which isn't actually true and that's the problem and actually what i do is i see from the other side which is many people they want to know the techniques of the person the injection techniques of the person who's presented an amazing before and after which is hugely about the production of that photograph and that creates a disconnect because

obviously you're trying to buy one thing which is the technique and i hope it'll give you something that's actually not you're not going to get with the technique you're going to have to work on your photography skills so you know i don't mind i can be non-judgmental about how you play that but you should know the process like let's know the game is create a beautiful image for instagram um and try and do you should do it in a way that's ethical if you're a healthcare practitioner but know that that's one of your skill sets to master for 2021. do you have any insights about how we might master that skill probably one of the most simple things you can do to improve is just use good light yeah use it in both before and the after a good light makes a really big difference um you know we i don't know how much of this is my brain but i you know i can tell when something's badly lit in one photo and well lit in the second picture it's instantly a lighting thing i think probably many people are uh just think it's the result but in terms of taking a good picture of a nose or lips you know a bright light on a young person with good skin creates a beautiful image in virtually everyone regardless of before and after

especially if you can see their eyes as well i always like to try and get a catch light in the eyes so you get a little glint in the eye and it makes people more beautiful you're highlighting the natural youth and beauty that they have and then you have a before and after in those contexts it just looks much better but lighting's probably the one that's easiest to solve get one of those um catch lights from around lights from amazon i think at 20 to 40 pounds and your pictures will instantly look better so do you have any other predictions for 2021 well i think on the horizon we have some negative stuff that's probably going to happen which is uh we have a basically a booming not unregulated market now with everyone there's a spectrum doesn't matter whether you talk about someone's height or their tendency to commit crime or their tendency to give to charity there's a spectrum of for everything and where you have a lack of regulation like we do in the uk that it leaves room for a section at the bottom end to do some pretty awful stuff and i think we're going to see some horrible

complications rea some really awful stuff because i sense the scale is really going up you know it's there's volume there's a volume of unregulated injectors and you know it's not to say that they're all bad some of them might be amazing way better than me but there's a spectrum isn't there so on the far end of that there's nothing to stop people who really shouldn't be injecting from injecting because we have no regulation and that's going to end up with some people getting some awful injuries i know that there are cases of blindness in the uk that are still unreported we're going to see more of that kind of thing unfortunately and if there's anything that good that comes out of it eventually is that we should we need to notice it talk about it more it's very stressful talking about it but it needs to be said and we need to then have a discussion as a country like what is the risk that we think the public should take when having injections do we need to put a system in place that protects the public or is it fine for everyone to make their own decision and get what they get um and so i think there's going to be a lot more of that and hopefully a couple of more big

changes in terms of what the healthcare practitioners are doing within their governing bodies so i'm hoping that there'll be a bit more regulation on whether you can prescribe commercially for people who have no medical training to inject drugs like i would hope that the gmc gdc those big bodies can start to have that discussion and lay out um and decrease the risk for the public that some of their members are taking by offload letting other people take the risk with a prescription that they've made so prescribers prescribing for non-meds yes okay you think they might you hope or you think there might be some tightening up uh i think there's definitely movement to try and create something like that now i know this from talking to the jccp that they're trying to that there are a lot of little tiny areas that you can improve that kind of have this collective effect of changing how everything runs so there's the big top down like we're going to ban people

who don't have whatever from injecting and then there are lots of little things in between i think they're going to be some of those shifts in the background that you can no longer operate with impunity and prescribe for everyone you know remotely they did it with remote prescribing as one example they stopped remote prescribing well it would be quite easy for them to do something similar for prescribing for commercially for unregulated people to inject their friends and family and colleagues whoever else so are you optimistic or pessimistic for 2021 and the aesthetic businesses we have uh i'm actually i'm inherently optimistic about the world generally i think we're going to have some struggles but people it's something i learned from being a gp actually people will get over almost anything and stuff that they think will absolutely crush them they bounce back from in less time than you'd think and that's not to diminish the scale of the difficulty for many people but i believe that humans are very good at bouncing back so i'm always optimistic let us know in the comments whether you think 2021 will be a good or a bad year

for aesthetics and we wish you all very best a wonderful new year happy new year thank you so much for watching us this year we started in we started this show in february i think and we've done many shows since we've had so many new subscribers and we're super grateful to you guys so thank you for your attention and for engaging with us thanks for watching all the best for 2021. [Music]
